data.title: OPENING SCENE
blocks: · Inventory all files in the folder.
· Identify duplicates and outdated drafts.
· Propose a naming convention.
· Apply the convention to all files.
· Remove duplicates and outdated drafts.

S03.meta: // SLIDE 03 — A PLAN IS A PROPOSAL, NOT PROO

data.title: A PLAN IS A PROPOSAL, NOT PROOF.
blocks: Agentic AI systems, including Cowork, can produce plans that are internally coherent but brittle — plans that handle the
A Plan Is a Proposal, Not Proof — This matches a well-established principle from automation research: humans must remain engaged enough to intervene (Bain
data.section: A PLAN IS A PROPOSAL, NOT PROO

data.title: THE PLAN-REVIEW CHECKLIST.
blocks: · Does the plan match the objective in my brief?
· Are there steps that are not connected to the objective?
· Has Cowork made assumptions about scope, audience, or priority that I did not specify?
· Are the source files or folders named or inventoried in the plan?
· Does the plan respect the allowed sources in my brief?

S05.meta: // SLIDE 05 — FOUR RESPONSES TO A PLAN

data.title: FOUR RESPONSES TO A PLAN.
blocks: Approve.
Redirect.
Pause.
Stop.

data.title: REDIRECTION IN PRACTICE.
blocks: When a plan needs revision, a redirection instruction has three parts: what to keep, what to change, and the new constra
Redirection in Practice — Here is the redirection Kenji should have written for the folder-organization plan:

data.title: WORKED WALKTHROUGH — REPORT PLAN REVIEW
blocks: Goal fit yes — all steps connect to the board summary objective.
Source coverage step 1 mentions the correct folder; the exclusions (draft files, template files) are not mentioned. Problem.
Risky steps no file moves, no deletions, no external submission. Writing a new file is low-risk. Acceptable.
Missing verification there is no step where Priya sees the source inventory and proposed structure before the draft is written. Problem.
Stop conditions no mention of what happens if financial figures are absent. Problem.

data.title: COMPUTER USE AND HIGH-SUPERVISION PLANS
blocks: · Require each step to be shown before it is executed (Anthropic, "Let Claude use your computer in Cowork" ).
· Explicitly prohibit form submissions, purchases, and account actions unless each is pre-approved.
· Name the specific sites or applications Cowork is permitted to interact with.
· Keep sensitive windows closed and unrelated applications hidden during the session.

S09.meta: // SLIDE 09 — THE HUMAN GATE FOR THIS CHAPTE

data.title: THE HUMAN GATE FOR THIS CHAPTER.
blocks: Plan review is where your knowledge of the work — knowledge Cowork cannot have — enters the process before any action is
The Human Gate for This Chapter — The plan review checklist gives your knowledge a place to land. Working through the checklist is not bureaucratic cautio
data.section: THE HUMAN GATE FOR THIS CHAPTE

data.title: COMMON MISTAKES
blocks: Approving because the plan sounds reasonable.
Treating redirection as evidence of failure.
Approving once and not monitoring.
Skipping the risky-step check for "simple" tasks.
Not reading the stop conditions.

data.title: AI WAYBACK MACHINE.
blocks: Lucy Suchman
Run this:
"Lucy Suchman plans and situated action"
Now make the prompt better.

data.title: THE CORE CLAIM.
blocks: Lucy Suchman wrote Plans and Situated Actions in 1987, arguing that plans are not scripts that people follow step by ste
Cowork's proposed plan is a resource, not a script. Your plan review is the situated judgment.
